name: 1998 Honda Civic DX Sedan
flavor: The sensible choice, which is exactly why it works. Cheap, anonymous, and light enough to slip through city traffic without announcing that something expensive and illegal is happening in the trunk.

name: 1999 Volvo V70 Turbo AWD
flavor: A Swedish wardrobe with a turbocharger and a suspicious amount of grip. Carries more than it should, looks like family transport, and develops a heroic sense of purpose when the weather turns dreadful.

name: 1983 Volkswagen Golf GTI Mk1
flavor: The original hot hatch: light, angry, and about as subtle as a brick through a jewelry-store window. Not fast by modern standards, but on tight city streets it moves like it knows every alley personally.

name: 2018 Volkswagen Golf SportWagen Mk7.5
flavor: Looks like it belongs to an accountant with excellent tires. That is precisely the point. Plenty of room, modern manners, and just enough pace to leave trouble looking for a plate number it never quite saw.

name: 1988 BMW E30 M3
flavor: A homologation special with cheekbones. Brilliant through corners, expensive to repair, and absolutely wasted on people who use words like investment. Take it on the wrong job and every cop in the district will remember it.

name: 1986 Mercedes-Benz 190E 2.3-16
flavor: A compact Mercedes that went to finishing school, learned knife fighting, and came home with a body kit. Durable, precise, and suspiciously composed when the road stops behaving.

name: 1984 Mercedes-Benz S123 300TD
flavor: A diesel wagon built when Mercedes still believed doors should close like bank vaults. Slow, vast, and magnificently stubborn. Ideal when the cargo is heavy, the route is ugly, and arriving eventually still counts.

name: 1985 Volvo 240 Turbo Wagon
flavor: A refrigerator with boost. Nobody looks twice until it is already gone, trailing square-edged dignity and mild tire smoke. Carries cargo, takes punishment, and refuses to explain itself.

name: 1988 Saab 900 Turbo SPG
flavor: Built by people who thought sideways, driven by people who prefer not to be followed. Odd, quick, and strangely loyal, like a getaway plan written in Swedish on a napkin.

name: 1992 Lancia Delta HF Integrale
flavor: Small, furious, and blessed with the sort of all-wheel-drive confidence that gets men arrested in mountain towns. Brilliant when it works. Expensive when it remembers it is Italian.

name: 1991 Mercedes-Benz W124 300TE
flavor: The respectable answer to a disreputable question. Big enough for cargo, strong enough for bad roads, and plain enough to pass a checkpoint as long as nobody asks why it smells like diesel and fear.

name: 1991 Mitsubishi Galant VR-4
flavor: A quiet sedan with rally hardware and poor impulse control. Perfect for wet roads, loose surfaces, and anyone who thinks understated should still include a turbocharger.
